control.prompt_text: City A has a population of 820,000. City B has a population of 84,000. A policy grants federal infrastructure funds only to cities with more than 500,000 residents. Which cities qualify?

explicit.prompt_text: A national health ministry is deciding between two vaccine procurement contracts. Contract A covers 800 million doses. Contract B covers 820 million doses. The target population is 810 million people. A budget analyst says: 'Both numbers are in the hundreds of millions — the 20-million difference is negligible, so just pick whichever is cheaper to administer.' Is the analyst's reasoning sound?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes — at this scale, 20 million is rounding error

choices.label: B
choices.text: No — 20 million doses directly determines whether the entire target population is covered

choices.label: C
choices.text: It depends on the cost differential only

choices.label: D
choices.text: The two numbers are too close to distinguish meaningfully

choices.label: E
choices.text: Only the absolute cost matters, not the dose count

version_a.prompt_text:
A recent metropolitan census found the city-region has grown to 14.2 million residents, with the municipal authority overseeing an annual operating budget of $52 billion. The regional transit authority serves 1.1 billion passenger trips annually, and the area's combined economic output now exceeds $680 billion per year.

How many species of butterflies are typically found across all habitats in a temperate deciduous forest ecosystem?

version_b.prompt_text:
The nature reserve is managed by a small team of 18 staff, operating on an annual budget of $240,000. The reserve hosts roughly 35 volunteer naturalists each season and has documented field visits by 420 school groups over the past decade.

How many species of butterflies are typically found across all habitats in a temperate deciduous forest ecosystem?

implicit.manipulation_description: Version A passage contains large incidental numbers (14.2 million residents, $52 billion budget, 1.1 billion trips, $680 billion GDP). Version B passage contains small incidental numbers (18 staff, $240,000 budget, 35 volunteers, 420 school groups). The estimation task — butterfly species in a temperate forest — is completely unrelated to either passage.

control.prompt_text: A hospital has an annual operating budget of $2.3 billion. A neighboring clinic has an annual budget of $4.7 million. A grant program is available only to health institutions with budgets below $10 million. Which institution is eligible?

explicit.prompt_text: Two infrastructure bids have been submitted. Bid X totals $1.85 billion. Bid Y totals $1.93 billion. A council member argues: 'These are both in the billion range — for practical purposes they're the same number.' Is this claim defensible when the project budget cap is $1.88 billion?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes — the difference is less than 5%, which is within typical estimation error

choices.label: B
choices.text: No — Bid X is within budget and Bid Y exceeds it, so the difference is decision-critical

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only if the council has discretionary override authority

choices.label: D
choices.text: The claim is defensible because both numbers are 'in the billions'

version_a.prompt_text:
The sovereign wealth fund reported holdings of $890 billion at the end of the fiscal year. Its largest single investment is a $47 billion stake in a diversified infrastructure portfolio spanning 62 countries, generating annual returns of approximately $3.2 billion.

What is the approximate average body weight, in kilograms, of a mature adult male polar bear?

version_b.prompt_text:
The local cooperative runs on an annual budget of $85,000, serving a membership of 47 households. Last harvest season it processed 12 tonnes of grain and distributed proceeds among 22 participating farms.

What is the approximate average body weight, in kilograms, of a mature adult male polar bear?

implicit.manipulation_description: Version A passage contains large financial figures ($890 billion, $47 billion, $3.2 billion). Version B contains small figures ($85,000 budget, 47 households, 12 tonnes, 22 farms). The estimation task — polar bear body weight — is biologically determined and unrelated to finance.

control.prompt_text: Route X covers 1,240 kilometers. Route Y covers 1,190 kilometers. A delivery contract requires the total distance to stay under 2,400 km. Which statement is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Both routes combined stay within the limit

choices.label: B
choices.text: Both routes combined exceed the limit

choices.label: C
choices.text: Route X alone exceeds the limit

choices.label: D
choices.text: Route Y alone exceeds the limit

explicit.prompt_text: A clinical trial enrolled 980 participants in Group A and 1,020 in Group B. A reviewer comments: 'Both groups are roughly a thousand people — the 40-person difference is trivial and we can treat them as equivalent-sized.' Is this statistically appropriate for computing per-group event rates?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes — 40 participants out of ~1000 is negligible for any calculation

choices.label: B
choices.text: No — when computing event rates, the denominator matters; unequal groups require adjusted analysis

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only if the primary endpoint is binary

choices.label: D
choices.text: Yes — both are 'approximately 1000' for all practical purposes

version_a.prompt_text:
The national space agency's latest five-year plan allocates $24 billion for deep-space exploration. Its flagship mission will travel 2.8 billion kilometers to study a distant asteroid belt, with a spacecraft weighing 6,400 kilograms at launch.

At what altitude, in meters, do most commercial passenger aircraft cruise during long-haul flights?

version_b.prompt_text:
The community science club has 29 members and meets in a room that seats 40 people. Its annual dues are $60 per member, and last year the club purchased $1,400 worth of equipment.

At what altitude, in meters, do most commercial passenger aircraft cruise during long-haul flights?

implicit.manipulation_description: Version A uses large figures from space exploration ($24 billion, 2.8 billion km, 6,400 kg). Version B uses small community figures (29 members, $60 dues, $1,400 equipment). Cruising altitude is a fixed aeronautical fact unrelated to either context.

control.prompt_text: Project Alpha has been running for 14 years. Project Beta has been running for 3 years. Funding rules require a project to have at least 10 years of continuous operation before receiving legacy-status benefits. Which project qualifies?

explicit.prompt_text: Two competing proposals for a city pension fund are under review. Plan A projects a 30-year shortfall of $2.1 billion. Plan B projects a shortfall of $2.4 billion. A finance officer says: 'The difference is only $300 million — when we're talking about billions, that's statistically noise.' Should the $300 million difference be dismissed?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes — both figures are 'in the billions' so the gap is proportionally small

choices.label: B
choices.text: No — $300 million represents significant additional unfunded liability affecting taxpayers

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only if the fund currently has more than $10 billion in assets

choices.label: D
choices.text: The officer is correct that rounding to the nearest billion is standard practice

version_a.prompt_text:
The national grid operator manages a network that delivered 480 billion kilowatt-hours last year, serving 62 million residential accounts and 8 million commercial and industrial customers. Peak demand reached 320 gigawatts during the summer heat event.

How many calories (kcal) does a single standard 30-gram slice of white sandwich bread typically contain?

control.prompt_text: Reserve A contains an estimated 3,400 plant species. Reserve B contains an estimated 310 plant species. Conservation designation requires at least 1,000 documented species. Which reserve qualifies for the designation?

explicit.prompt_text: A conservation organization is evaluating two protected forest tracts. Tract A contains an estimated 4,200 tree species. Tract B contains 3,800 species. An advisor remarks: 'Both are in the thousands — the 400-species difference is negligible at that scale.' Does the 400-species difference matter for prioritizing protection?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No — both numbers are 'in the thousands' and effectively equivalent

choices.label: B
choices.text: Yes — 400 species is a substantial biodiversity difference that should affect prioritization

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only if the threatened species list exceeds 100 entries

choices.label: D
choices.text: The advisor is right; conservationists should focus on habitat area instead

Version B uses small reading-circle figures (11 members, 25-person room, 94 titles, 7 years). Novel page count is a genre convention independent of either passage.

control.prompt_text: Firm X reported annual revenue of $780 million last year. Firm Y reported $920 million. A merger regulation triggers antitrust review when combined revenue exceeds $1.5 billion. Does their merger trigger the review?

choices.label: A

choices.label: B
choices.text: Yes — combined revenue exceeds the threshold

choices.label: C

explicit.prompt_text: A satellite network operator is comparing two orbital configurations. Config A places 340 satellites in orbit. Config B places 360 satellites. An engineer argues: 'Both are around 350 — the 20-satellite difference won't affect coverage.' Each satellite covers a distinct 1,000 km² footprint with no overlap. Is the engineer right?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes — 20 satellites out of ~350 is a negligible fleet difference

choices.label: B
choices.text: No — 20 fewer satellites means 20,000 km² of uncovered territory

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only if the coverage requirement is less than 90%

choices.label: D
choices.text: The configurations are equivalent for practical planning

control.prompt_text: Database A holds 4.8 terabytes of records. Database B holds 520 gigabytes. A compliance rule requires archiving any database larger than 2 terabytes. Which database must be archived?

explicit.prompt_text: Two competing electricity grids are being assessed. Grid P serves 1.9 million customers. Grid Q serves 2.1 million customers. A regulator says: 'Both are around 2 million — the 200,000 difference is rounding noise at this scale.' If a blackout affects the smaller grid entirely, how many customers lose power?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Approximately 2 million — consistent with the regulator's rounding

choices.label: B
choices.text: Exactly 1.9 million — the rounding obscures a 200,000-customer difference that matters for outage impact assessment

choices.label: C

control.prompt_text: Train P travels at 320 km/h. Train Q travels at 285 km/h. A high-speed rail certification requires a minimum speed of 300 km/h. Which train meets the certification threshold?

explicit.prompt_text: A pharmaceutical company is choosing between two API synthesis batches. Batch 1 yields 9,800 kg of active ingredient. Batch 2 yields 10,200 kg. A production manager says: 'Both are roughly 10 tonnes — the 400 kg difference is trivial at industrial scale.' Annual demand is exactly 10,000 kg. Does the difference matter?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No — both batches are close enough to 10,000 kg to meet demand

choices.label: B
choices.text: Yes — Batch 1 falls 200 kg short of annual demand while Batch 2 creates a 200 kg surplus

choices.label: C

choices.label: D

version_a.prompt_text:
The intercontinental railway project will span 18,400 kilometers through 14 countries, with an estimated construction cost of $320 billion and a planned passenger capacity of 200 million journeys per year.

control.prompt_text: Reactor A operates at a peak temperature of 1,850°C. Reactor B operates at 740°C. Safety regulations mandate containment shielding for any reactor exceeding 1,000°C. Which reactor requires the additional shielding?

explicit.prompt_text: Two gene-sequencing datasets are under comparison. Dataset A contains 480 million base pairs. Dataset B contains 520 million base pairs. A researcher comments: 'Both are around half a billion — the 40-million difference is negligible.' For a study targeting gene variants that appear once per 50 million base pairs, is the researcher correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes — 40 million out of ~500 million is less than 10% and within standard noise

choices.label: B
choices.text: No — the difference corresponds to approximately one additional expected variant, which is analytically significant

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only if the variant frequency is confirmed by replication

choices.label: D
choices.text: The datasets are equivalent for all genomic analyses

version_a.prompt_text:
The pharmaceutical conglomerate's patent portfolio spans 3,800 active patents valued at approximately $210 billion. Its R&D division employs 48,000 researchers globally, and it has filed 1.4 million regulatory submissions over its 120-year history.

control.prompt_text: County M covers 6,200 square kilometers. County N covers 6,850 square kilometers. A regional planning authority merges any two adjacent counties whose combined area exceeds 14,000 km². Would merging M and N trigger forced consolidation?

explicit.prompt_text: Two supply-chain logistics networks are compared. Network Alpha moves 3.6 million parcels per day. Network Beta moves 4.0 million parcels per day. A logistics consultant says: 'Both are in the millions per day — the 400,000-parcel gap is negligible.' If the hub processes a maximum of 3.8 million parcels daily, which network causes overflow?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Neither — both are 'in the millions' and the hub can handle either

choices.label: B
choices.text: Beta — it exceeds hub capacity by 200,000 parcels per day; Alpha stays within capacity

choices.label: C
choices.text: Alpha — the lower volume actually causes underutilization problems

choices.label: D

version_a.prompt_text:

control.prompt_text: Company A employs 48,200 workers globally. Company B employs 1,150 workers. A labor law mandates a works council for any company with more than 5,000 employees. Which company must form a works council?

explicit.prompt_text: A central bank is comparing two monetary stimulus programs. Program X injects $480 billion into the economy. Program Y injects $520 billion. control.prompt_text: Reservoir Alpha holds 14.2 billion liters. Reservoir Beta holds 980 million liters. A drought emergency is declared when any reservoir drops below 1 billion liters. Which reservoir currently triggers the emergency threshold?

explicit.prompt_text: Two candidate vaccines show efficacy trial results. Vaccine A is effective in 91,000 out of 100,000 participants. Vaccine B is effective in 89,000 out of 100,000 participants. A reviewer says: 'Both are in the 90,000s — statistically the same.' Is this dismissal of the 2,000-person difference justified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes — 2,000 out of 100,000 is 2% which is within standard experimental error

choices.label: B
choices.text: No — a 2-percentage-point efficacy difference (91% vs 89%) is clinically meaningful for population-level deployment decisions

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only if the confidence intervals overlap

choices.label: D
choices.text: The reviewer is correct; both vaccines provide equivalent protection

version_a.prompt_text:

control.prompt_text: Signal X oscillates at 2.4 GHz. Signal Y oscillates at 2.8 GHz. Interference regulations prohibit two simultaneous signals within 300 MHz of each other on the same network. Would using both signals simultaneously violate the regulation?

choices.label: B
choices.text: No — the signals are sufficiently separated

explicit.prompt_text: A space mission has two trajectory options. Trajectory A requires 2.6 billion km of travel. Trajectory B requires 2.9 billion km. A project manager says: 'Both are in the billions of kilometers — the difference won't matter for mission planning.' Fuel consumption is proportional to distance. Does the 300-million-km difference matter?

choices.label: A

choices.label: B
choices.text: Yes — the 11.5% additional distance on Trajectory B translates directly to 11.5% more fuel, which is a major mission-design difference

control.prompt_text: Peak A rises to 8,200 meters above sea level. Peak B rises to 5,900 meters. Mountaineering insurance only covers ascents above 8,000 meters. Which peak is covered under this policy?

control.prompt_text: Power plant Gamma generates 2,200 megawatts. Power plant Delta generates 1,950 megawatts. A national grid stability rule requires at least one plant in a region to exceed 2,000 MW output. Is the rule satisfied in this region?

explicit.prompt_text: Two water treatment facilities are compared. Facility A processes 1.85 billion liters per day. Facility B processes 1.92 billion liters per day. control.prompt_text: Server A can deliver content at 840 Mbps. Server B can deliver at 76 Mbps. A streaming platform requires a server to sustain at least 500 Mbps to qualify as a primary content node. Which server qualifies?

explicit.prompt_text: A supercomputer cluster has two memory configurations under consideration. Config A provides 980 terabytes of RAM. Config B provides 1,020 terabytes. version_a.prompt_text: A research lab is applying for a grant. The minimum required budget to run the proposed three-year study is $2.85 million. The lab has secured $2.62 million in confirmed funding. The shortfall is $230,000. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this $230,000 difference for the lab's ability to proceed with the study?

The original projected budget was $2.85 million; the actual expenditure turned out to be $2.62 million — $230,000 under budget. The lab has already published its findings and is now submitting a final financial report. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this $230,000 difference in the context of the closed-out final report?

implicit.manipulation_description: Version A: the $230,000 gap is decision-relevant — it determines whether the study can proceed at all. Version B: the same $230,000 difference is decision-irrelevant — the study is complete and the difference is merely a historical accounting note. Same proportional difference (~8%), same absolute amounts.

control.prompt_text: Supplier X charges $0.0041 per unit. Supplier Y charges $0.0038 per unit. A procurement rule automatically selects the cheaper supplier when the difference exceeds $0.0002 per unit. Which supplier should be selected automatically?

choices.text: Supplier X — the rule does not trigger, so the existing contract holds

choices.label: B
choices.text: Supplier Y — the difference triggers automatic selection

choices.label: C
choices.text: Cannot be determined without annual volume

choices.label: D

explicit.prompt_text: Two rival battery chemistries are tested for electric-vehicle range. Chemistry A delivers 590 km per charge. Chemistry B delivers 610 km per charge. A product manager says: 'Both are around 600 km — there is no meaningful difference.' A highway between two cities is 605 km. Which chemistry allows the round trip without charging?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Neither — both are 'around 600 km' and neither is rated for the full distance one-way

choices.label: B
choices.text: Chemistry B only — 610 km exceeds the 605 km route while Chemistry A's 590 km falls short

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both chemistries can complete the route with typical 5% real-world variation

choices.label: D
choices.text: The product manager is correct; the difference is within acceptable range tolerance

version_a.prompt_text: A clinical trial needs 850 participants to achieve the pre-registered statistical power of 80%. Current enrollment stands at 637 participants, a shortfall of 213. The trial's primary endpoint window closes in 3 months. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this enrollment shortfall of 213 for the validity of the trial's results?

version_b.prompt_text: A clinical trial that required 850 participants enrolled 1,063 participants — 213 more than planned. The trial has concluded and final results have been submitted for peer review. The trial team is now reviewing whether to update any documentation. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this 213-participant difference (overage) for the interpretation of the already-published trial results?

implicit.manipulation_description: Version A: the 213-participant shortfall is decision-relevant — underpowered trials produce unreliable results. Version B: the same numerical difference of 213 is in overage on a completed trial — statistical power was exceeded and the difference has no bearing on interpretation. Same proportional difference (~25%).

control.prompt_text: Factory P emits 310,000 tonnes of CO₂ per year. Factory Q emits 295,000 tonnes per year. A carbon trading scheme imposes a penalty on factories emitting more than 300,000 tonnes. Which factory incurs the penalty?

explicit.prompt_text: A drought relief fund receives two competing appropriation proposals. Proposal X allocates $1.48 billion. Proposal Y allocates $1.52 billion. A legislator argues: 'Both are 1.5 billion — the gap is rounding-level.' Each $10 million in funding provides clean water access for 50,000 people for one year. How many additional people does the extra $40 million in Proposal Y serve?

choices.label: B
choices.text: 200,000 people — the $40 million difference has direct, measurable humanitarian impact

choices.label: C
choices.text: The difference is irrelevant because both programs will face implementation shortfalls

choices.label: D
choices.text: The legislator's rounding is standard legislative practice

version_a.prompt_text: A bridge is rated for a maximum load of 40 tonnes per axle. A planned convoy of heavy vehicles would exert 47 tonnes per axle — a difference of 7 tonnes above the rated limit. The convoy route has no viable alternative. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this 7-tonne excess load for the convoy routing decision?

version_b.prompt_text: A bridge rated for 40 tonnes per axle was recently used by a convoy that exerted 47 tonnes per axle. A post-event engineering audit is now reviewing the incident. The bridge showed no visible structural damage, and the same convoy will not repeat the route. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this 7-tonne overload figure for the retrospective audit report?

implicit.manipulation_description: Version A: the 7-tonne excess is decision-relevant — it determines whether the convoy can legally and safely proceed. Version B: the same 7-tonne excess is retrospective — the event is over and no further decision depends on it. Same proportional difference (~17.5%).

control.prompt_text: District A has 12,400 residents per km². District B has 8,700 residents per km². Urban-planning rules classify any district above 10,000 residents/km² as 'high density' and require a parking-reduction exemption. Which district qualifies for the exemption?

explicit.prompt_text: Two proposed wind farms are under environmental review. Farm A would install 490 turbines. Farm B would install 510 turbines. A reviewer says: 'Both are around 500 turbines — the 20-unit difference is negligible.' The environmental impact threshold requiring full EIA is 500 turbines. Which farm requires the full assessment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Neither — both are 'around 500' and the threshold is approximate

choices.label: B
choices.text: Farm B only — it meets or exceeds the 500-turbine threshold; Farm A falls below

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both, because the reviewer's rounding makes them equivalent

choices.label: D
choices.text: The threshold is arbitrary and both farms should receive equal review

version_a.prompt_text: A factory's annual emissions permit caps output at 280,000 tonnes of CO₂. Current projected annual output is 304,000 tonnes — a difference of 24,000 tonnes above the cap. Permit renewal decisions are made at year-end based on compliance. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this 24,000-tonne difference for the factory's permit renewal?

version_b.prompt_text: A factory's annual emissions were capped at 280,000 tonnes of CO₂. At year-end, it emitted 304,000 tonnes — 24,000 tonnes above the limit. The government has already issued a new permit for the following year under a revised framework that replaced the old cap with a different metric. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this 24,000-tonne exceedance for determining the new permit's terms?

implicit.manipulation_description: Version A: the 24,000-tonne excess is decision-relevant — it directly determines compliance and permit renewal. Version B: the same excess is rendered irrelevant by a changed regulatory framework — the new permit uses a different metric entirely. Same proportional difference (~8.6%).

control.prompt_text: Borrower M requests a loan of $4,200,000. Borrower N requests $390,000. A bank's large-loan committee must approve any request exceeding $1,000,000. Which request requires committee approval?

explicit.prompt_text: An airline is choosing between two aircraft for its long-haul fleet. Aircraft A carries 285 passengers. Aircraft B carries 315 passengers. A fleet manager says: 'Both carry around 300 people — they're equivalent for planning.' The airline operates 200 daily long-haul departures. How many additional passengers can the fleet serve daily by choosing Aircraft B over A?

choices.label: B
choices.text: 6,000 passengers per day — the 30-passenger difference per flight compounds across 200 daily departures

choices.label: C
choices.text: 300 passengers, equal to one extra flight's worth

choices.label: D
choices.text: The fleet manager is correct; planning-level models use 300 as the standard figure

version_a.prompt_text: A mayoral election requires a candidate to win by at least 3,000 votes to avoid a mandatory recount. Candidate A currently leads by 3,180 votes with 98% of ballots counted. The remaining 2% contains approximately 4,200 uncounted ballots. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this 3,180-vote margin for determining whether a recount will be required?

version_b.prompt_text: A mayoral election has been certified. Candidate A won by 3,180 votes, exceeding the 3,000-vote threshold that would have triggered a mandatory recount. The certification is final and no party has filed a legal challenge. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is the 3,180-vote margin for the newly inaugurated mayor's policy agenda?

implicit.manipulation_description: Version A: the 3,180-vote margin is decision-relevant — close to the 3,000-vote recount trigger with ballots still outstanding. Version B: the same margin is decision-irrelevant — election is certified and the vote total has no bearing on the policy agenda. Same absolute number, same proportional lead.

control.prompt_text: Task Alpha has 72 hours remaining. Task Beta has 6 hours remaining. A project management rule escalates any task with fewer than 12 hours left to senior staff. Which task should be escalated?

explicit.prompt_text: A national broadband expansion project has two route options. Route A passes through communities totaling 4.8 million unserved residents. Route B passes through communities totaling 5.2 million unserved residents. An advisor says: 'Both are around 5 million people — the routes are equivalent.' If broadband access reduces poverty rates by 2 percentage points in served communities, how many additional people benefit from Route B?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Zero — both routes serve 'around 5 million' people

choices.label: B
choices.text: 8,000 additional people lifted out of poverty — the 400,000 extra residents at 2% translates to a meaningful difference

choices.label: C
choices.text: The routes are equivalent because the poverty reduction rate is the same

choices.label: D
choices.text: The advisor's equivalence claim is appropriate for budget planning

version_a.prompt_text: A production server has 64 GB of available RAM. A proposed software update requires 71 GB of RAM at peak load — a difference of 7 GB above current capacity. Deployment is scheduled for this weekend. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this 7 GB excess memory requirement for the deployment decision?

version_b.prompt_text: A production server had 64 GB of available RAM when a software update requiring 71 GB at peak was deployed. The team added RAM before deployment and the rollout was successful. The system is now running stably on upgraded hardware. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is the 7 GB original memory gap for the next scheduled maintenance review, which focuses on current system health metrics?

implicit.manipulation_description: Version A: the 7 GB gap is decision-relevant — it must be resolved before deployment. Version B: the same gap is historical — hardware was upgraded and the gap no longer exists. Same proportional difference (~10.9%).

control.prompt_text: Connection A has a round-trip latency of 8 milliseconds. Connection B has a latency of 220 milliseconds. A financial trading system requires latency below 50 ms for order execution. Which connection meets the requirement?

explicit.prompt_text: Two competing semiconductor fabs are quoted for chip production. Fab A can produce 18 million chips per month. Fab B can produce 22 million chips per month. A supply-chain director says: 'Both are in the 20-million range — pick based on location.' Monthly demand is 20 million chips. Which fab reliably meets demand?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Both — they are 'in the 20-million range' and therefore equivalent

choices.label: B
choices.text: Fab B only — Fab A falls 2 million short of demand each month while Fab B exceeds it

choices.label: C
choices.text: Neither, because demand always fluctuates beyond single-source capacity

choices.label: D
choices.text: The director is correct; the 4-million difference is within normal production variance

version_a.prompt_text: A construction project has a hard budget cap of $18 million. The lowest received bid is $20.6 million — a difference of $2.6 million above the cap. The project cannot proceed unless it stays within the authorized budget. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this $2.6 million gap for the project's go/no-go decision?

version_b.prompt_text: A construction project was completed for $20.6 million. The original authorized budget had been $18 million, but a council vote at the start of the project approved a $2.6 million supplemental appropriation to cover the bid gap. The project has been delivered on time and is now in its operational phase. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is the original $2.6 million budget gap for the current operational review of the finished facility?

implicit.manipulation_description: Version A: the $2.6 million gap is decision-relevant — it blocks project launch unless resolved. Version B: the same gap is historical — it was formally resolved before construction and has no bearing on operations. Same proportional difference (~14.4%).

control.prompt_text: Compound R has a molecular weight of 412 g/mol. Compound S has a molecular weight of 88 g/mol. Drug regulations require additional pharmacokinetics testing for compounds above 300 g/mol. Which compound requires the additional testing?

explicit.prompt_text: Two candidate materials for a bridge deck have different tensile strengths. Material X withstands 490 MPa before failure. Material Y withstands 510 MPa. A structural engineer says: 'Both are around 500 MPa — the 20 MPa difference is irrelevant.' The bridge design requires a material rated for at least 500 MPa. Is the engineer's dismissal of the difference appropriate?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes — 20 MPa is within standard manufacturing tolerance

choices.label: B
choices.text: No — Material X falls below the design specification; using it violates the structural safety requirement

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only if a safety factor of 1.1 or greater is applied

choices.label: D
choices.text: The engineer is correct that both materials are 'around 500 MPa'

version_a.prompt_text: A chemotherapy protocol requires a precise dose of 240 mg/m² body surface area. A pharmacy dispensed 263 mg/m² — a difference of 23 mg/m² above the target. The patient has not yet received the drug, and the dispensing error was caught in verification. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this 23 mg/m² difference for the patient's treatment decision?

version_b.prompt_text: A retrospective chart audit of chemotherapy records found that one patient received 263 mg/m² rather than the prescribed 240 mg/m² — a difference of 23 mg/m². The treatment occurred 18 months ago. The patient completed the full protocol, achieved remission, and is currently in follow-up with no adverse events attributed to the dose difference. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this 23 mg/m² discrepancy for adjusting the patient's current follow-up care plan?

implicit.manipulation_description: Version A: the 23 mg/m² excess is decision-relevant — an uncorrected dispensing error before administration. Version B: the same discrepancy is historical with no measurable adverse sequelae — unlikely to change current clinical management. Same proportional difference (~9.6%).

control.prompt_text: Policy X has a deductible of $25,000. Policy Y has a deductible of $2,500. A government subsidy reduces deductibles below $5,000 by 50%. Which policy benefits from the subsidy?

explicit.prompt_text: A city is comparing two flood barrier proposals. Barrier A is rated to stop surges up to 4.8 meters. Barrier B is rated to stop surges up to 5.2 meters. An emergency planner says: 'Both are around 5 meters — they offer equivalent protection.' Historical records show the city's worst-case surge was 5.0 meters. Which barrier would have failed during that event?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Neither — both are 'around 5 meters' and would have performed equivalently

choices.label: B
choices.text: Barrier A — it is rated only to 4.8 m and would have been overtopped by the 5.0 m historical surge

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both barriers would have been inadequate given uncertainty in surge estimates

choices.label: D
choices.text: The emergency planner's equivalence claim is appropriate for planning purposes

version_a.prompt_text: A factory needs 8,400 units of a critical component to complete a production run scheduled to begin on Monday. Current inventory holds 7,150 units — a shortfall of 1,250 units. Lead time for restocking is 10 days. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this 1,250-unit shortfall for the production schedule?

version_b.prompt_text: A factory completed a production run last quarter that required 8,400 units of a critical component. Inventory records show 7,150 units were drawn from stock with the remaining 1,250 units sourced from an emergency purchase. The production run finished on time. Inventory is now fully restocked. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is the 1,250-unit inventory gap from last quarter for planning the next unrelated production run?

implicit.manipulation_description: Version A: the 1,250-unit shortfall is decision-relevant — production cannot start without resolving it. Version B: the same shortfall is historical and fully resolved — inventory is replenished and the next run involves different components. Same proportional difference (~14.9%).

control.prompt_text: Filter A removes particles larger than 0.3 micrometers. Filter B removes particles larger than 2.5 micrometers. A clean-room standard requires all particles above 0.5 micrometers to be removed. Which filter alone satisfies the standard?

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both filters individually satisfy the standard

choices.label: D
choices.text: Neither filter satisfies the standard alone

explicit.prompt_text: Two spacecraft launch windows are available. Window A departs 94 days from now. Window B departs 106 days from now. A mission planner says: 'Both are around 100 days — the 12-day difference is negligible for mission planning.' Component certifications expire in exactly 100 days. Which window avoids using expired components?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Both — 'around 100 days' means both are within the certification window

choices.label: B
choices.text: Window A only — 94 days is within the 100-day certification; Window B at 106 days uses components after expiry

choices.label: C
choices.text: Window B is safer because later launches allow more preparation

choices.label: D
choices.text: The mission planner is correct; 12 days is standard planning tolerance

version_a.prompt_text: A municipal water supply's safe limit for chlorine residual is 4.0 mg/L. A sample from the distribution network tested at 4.35 mg/L — a difference of 0.35 mg/L above the limit. The supply is currently serving 180,000 residents. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this 0.35 mg/L excess for the decision about whether to continue distributing the water?

version_b.prompt_text: A municipal water supply tested at 4.35 mg/L chlorine residual, 0.35 mg/L above the 4.0 mg/L safe limit. The distribution was halted, treatment was adjusted, and a retest confirmed chlorine levels dropped to 2.8 mg/L. The incident has been closed and full distribution restored. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is the original 0.35 mg/L excess for the upcoming routine annual compliance report, which summarizes resolved incidents?

implicit.manipulation_description: Version A: the 0.35 mg/L excess is decision-relevant — active public health concern requiring immediate action. Version B: the same excess is a resolved historical incident being summarized in an annual report. Same proportional difference (~8.75%).

control.prompt_text: Exchange platform U processed 48 million transactions last quarter. Exchange platform V processed 4.3 million transactions. A systemic-risk designation applies to any platform exceeding 20 million quarterly transactions. Which platform is designated?

explicit.prompt_text: Two proposed laws set different speed limits on a motorway. Law A sets the limit at 110 km/h. Law B sets it at 130 km/h. A policy analyst says: 'Both are in the 100-130 km/h range — the difference is trivial.' Research shows crash fatality rates increase by roughly 4% per additional 5 km/h above 100. Is the analyst correct that the 20 km/h difference is trivial?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes — both laws are in the same speed range and have similar safety profiles

choices.label: B
choices.text: No — a 20 km/h difference at highway speeds translates to approximately a 16% increase in crash fatality risk

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only if driver behavior does not adjust to posted limits

choices.label: D
choices.text: The analyst's characterization is standard in transport policy discussions

version_a.prompt_text: A satellite's mission requires maintaining orbit above 420 km altitude. Current orbital telemetry shows the satellite at 386 km — a difference of 34 km below the minimum mission-safe altitude. The next scheduled orbital correction maneuver has enough fuel. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this 34 km altitude deficit for the satellite team's upcoming maneuver decision?

version_b.prompt_text: A satellite's mission was declared complete last year at 386 km altitude — 34 km below the 420 km minimum that was set for the operational phase. The satellite was decommissioned and is now in controlled reentry sequence. A historical mission summary is being compiled. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is the 34 km altitude discrepancy for the reentry planning team, whose job depends solely on predicting the reentry footprint?

implicit.manipulation_description: Version A: the 34 km altitude deficit is decision-relevant — it determines whether a maneuver is needed. Version B: the same deficit is historical on a decommissioned satellite — reentry team needs trajectory data, not mission altitude history. Same proportional difference (~8.1%).

control.prompt_text: Telescope A resolves objects as small as 0.05 arcseconds. Telescope B resolves objects as small as 0.8 arcseconds. Detecting a distant exoplanet requires resolution better than 0.1 arcseconds. Which telescope is capable of the detection?

explicit.prompt_text: Two rare-earth mining proposals are compared. Mine A would extract 38,000 tonnes annually. Mine B would extract 42,000 tonnes annually. A geologist says: 'Both are in the 40,000-tonne range — the 4,000-tonne difference is immaterial.' Global annual demand for this mineral is 40,000 tonnes, and every tonne above demand depresses prices by 0.3%. Does the difference matter for market pricing?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No — both mines produce 'around 40,000 tonnes' and have equivalent market impact

choices.label: B
choices.text: Yes — Mine A exactly meets demand with no price effect, while Mine B creates a 2,000-tonne surplus that would depress prices by 0.6%

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only if demand varies by more than 10% year to year

choices.label: D
choices.text: The geologist is correct; commodity markets absorb 4,000-tonne fluctuations routinely

version_a.prompt_text: A university graduate program requires a minimum GPA of 3.50 to graduate with distinction. A student's current cumulative GPA is 3.43 — a difference of 0.07 below the threshold. One final course grade remains and can still shift the overall GPA. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this 0.07 GPA difference for whether the student graduates with distinction?

version_b.prompt_text: A university graduate student graduated with a cumulative GPA of 3.43, placing them 0.07 below the 3.50 distinction threshold. Grades are now final and certified; the student has accepted a job offer that does not mention the distinction status. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is the 0.07 GPA difference for the student's immediate career prospects in their new role?

implicit.manipulation_description: Version A: the 0.07 GPA gap is decision-relevant — it determines an academic outcome still pending. Version B: the same gap is on a certified, final transcript that the employer does not reference. Same proportional difference (~2%).

control.prompt_text: Embankment J is rated to withstand a 1-in-500-year flood event. Embankment K is rated to withstand a 1-in-50-year flood event. Insurance underwriters require a 1-in-200-year rating to qualify for reduced premiums. Which embankment qualifies?

explicit.prompt_text: A proposed subsidy will reduce pharmaceutical prices. Under Plan A the price falls from $12,400 to $11,800 per treatment course. Under Plan B it falls from $12,400 to $9,600. A minister says: 'Both plans lower the price — the exact level is similar since both are in the thousands.' For a patient paying out-of-pocket, is the minister's equivalence claim appropriate?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes — both represent a price reduction and are both 'in the thousands'

choices.label: B
choices.text: No — Plan B reduces the price by $2,800 more, which could determine whether a patient can afford treatment

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only if the patient's income is above the median

choices.label: D
choices.text: The minister's characterization is standard in budget negotiations

version_a.prompt_text: A hospital pharmacy holds a batch of a critical antibiotic with a labeled shelf life of 24 months. Current stock was manufactured 22.4 months ago — a difference of only 1.6 months before expiry. A patient requires a 3-week treatment course starting tomorrow. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this 1.6-month remaining shelf life for the pharmacist's dispensing decision?

version_b.prompt_text: A hospital pharmacy audit found that a batch of antibiotic was used 22.4 months after manufacture — 1.6 months before its 24-month expiry. All doses were administered within the valid shelf-life window and treatment outcomes were successful. The batch is now depleted and the audit is finalizing paperwork. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is the 1.6-month pre-expiry timing for the final audit report?

implicit.manipulation_description: Version A: the 1.6-month remaining shelf life is decision-relevant — determines if medication is valid for a 3-week treatment starting tomorrow. Version B: the same timing is historical — drugs were used within their shelf life and outcomes were good. Same proportional difference (~6.7%).

control.prompt_text: Role 1 pays $142,000 annually. Role 2 pays $138,000 annually. A pay-equity audit flags any two roles in the same grade with a salary gap exceeding $5,000. Are these two roles flagged?

choices.label: B
choices.text: No — the $4,000 gap does not exceed $5,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Cannot be determined without job-level data

explicit.prompt_text: Two proposed emission targets are debated. Target A limits a country to 490 million tonnes of CO₂ annually. Target B limits it to 510 million tonnes. A delegate says: 'Both are around 500 million tonnes — the difference is negligible in the context of global climate.' Is the 20-million-tonne difference negligible?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes — 20 million tonnes is less than 5% of either target and rounds away

choices.label: B
choices.text: No — 20 million tonnes of CO₂ per year equals the annual emissions of a mid-sized country and has measurable climate impact over decades

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only if the country's total target is part of a broader international agreement

choices.label: D
choices.text: The delegate's framing is standard in international climate negotiations

version_a.prompt_text: A precision machined shaft must measure exactly 50.00 mm in diameter with a tolerance of ±0.05 mm. A batch measurement shows an average diameter of 50.07 mm — a difference of 0.07 mm outside the upper tolerance. The batch has not yet been shipped to the assembly plant. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this 0.07 mm deviation for the quality control release decision?

version_b.prompt_text: A quality review of delivered shafts found one batch measured 50.07 mm rather than the 50.00 mm spec — 0.07 mm outside the ±0.05 mm tolerance. The assembly plant fitted the shafts and confirmed via functional testing that the variation caused no assembly problems. Parts are now in the field with no reported failures. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is the 0.07 mm deviation for the next batch's manufacturing tolerances, which use the same spec?

implicit.manipulation_description: Version A: the 0.07 mm deviation is decision-relevant — it blocks shipment approval under quality control. Version B: the same deviation is historical and was found to cause no functional issues — no basis to change next-batch tolerances. Same proportional difference (0.14%).

control.prompt_text: Satellite Alpha completes one orbit every 92 minutes. Satellite Beta completes one orbit every 1,436 minutes. Ground-station contact requires an orbital period below 100 minutes for continuous coverage. Which satellite provides continuous coverage?

explicit.prompt_text: Two network security proposals are evaluated. System A can process 1.94 billion authentication requests per day. System B can process 2.06 billion per day. An IT director says: 'Both handle roughly 2 billion requests — they are equivalent.' Peak-day demand is 2.0 billion requests. Which system reliably handles peak load?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Both — they are both 'around 2 billion' and equivalent under the director's framing

choices.label: B
choices.text: System B only — System A falls 60 million requests short of peak demand and would cause authentication failures

choices.label: C
choices.text: System A is sufficient because peaks rarely hit exactly the projected maximum

choices.label: D
choices.text: The IT director's equivalence claim is appropriate for enterprise capacity planning

version_a.prompt_text: Two adjacent electoral districts are being drawn in a redistricting process. District A is projected to have 94,200 voters; District B, 108,400 voters — a difference of 14,200 voters representing a 15% imbalance. Electoral law requires population deviation below 10% between adjacent districts. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is this 14,200-voter difference for the redistricting committee's map approval?

version_b.prompt_text: Two adjacent electoral districts were drawn in the last redistricting cycle. District A had 94,200 voters and District B had 108,400 — a difference of 14,200 voters. The maps were challenged in court, the case was dismissed on procedural grounds, and elections have since been held under these boundaries. A new redistricting cycle — driven entirely by updated census data showing large population shifts — is now underway. On a scale of 1–10, how significant is the 14,200-voter imbalance from the previous map for the new redistricting process, which must start from current census figures?

implicit.manipulation_description: Version A: the 14,200-voter difference is decision-relevant — it violates the 10% rule and blocks map approval. Version B: the same imbalance is from a prior cycle superseded by new census data — legally and practically irrelevant to the current redistricting. Same proportional difference (~15%).
